content.full_text:
870703(3)

75 205

May 7, 1975

Mrs. March Fong Eu
Secretary of State
111 Capitol Mall
Sacramento, CA 95814

Dear Mrs. Eu:

Re: Request for Opinion
    No. 75-106

Thank you for your letter of April 22, 1975
requesting an opinion with respect to your duties
under the Political Reform Act.

No opinion will be issued regarding the first
question because the period for "prompt" notice is
within the discretion of the Secretary of State.
Obviously, you should provide the notice as soon as
it is feasible.

No opinion will be issued regarding the first
part of the second question because the identical
question is before the Commission in Opinion Request
No. 75-24, a copy of which is enclosed. You are
invited to submit views on this question.

The second and third parts of the second question
raise no substantial question involving interpretation
of the Political Reform Act. The Act does not require
that notice be provided before a fine is imposed.

Although for the reasons stated no formal opinion
will be issued, I hope the foregoing is sufficiently
helpful. If you have any further questions, I urge
you to contact a member of the Commission staff.

                                    Sincerely,


                                    Daniel H. Lowenstein
                                    Chairman

DHL:cjb
Enclosure

Office of the Secretary of State
March Fong Eu

111 Capitol Mall
Sacramento, California 95814

Executive Office (916) 445-6371
Corporations (916) 445-1430
Corporation Indet (916) 445-2900
Corporation Records (916) 445-1768
Elections Division (916) 445-0820
Legal Division (Corp.) (916) 445-0520
Notary Public Division (916) 445-6507
State Archives (916) 445-4293
Uniform Commercial Code (916) 445-8061

Request No. 70 iO

April 22, 1975 Date Received 4-28
Response Due 5-12

Daniel H. Lowenstein
Chairman
Fair Political Practices Commission
926 J Street, Suite 522
Sacramento, CA 95814

Dear Mr. Lowenstein:

The Secretary of State requests the Fair Political Practices
Commission's opinion in response to the following:

Proposition 9, Government Code Section 81010(c) provides:
"With respect to reports and statements filed with her
pursuant to this title, the filing officer shall:
(c) Notify promptly all persons and known committees who
have failed to file a report or statement in the form
and at the time required by this title."

1. Notice within what period of time constitutes a "prompt" notice to be given by a filing officer to a late filer?

Proposition 9, Government Code Section 91013 provides:
"If any person files a statement or report, or a copy of a
statement or report, after any deadline imposed by this Act,
he shall, in addition to any other penalties or remedies
established by this Act, be liable to the filing officer or
other officer with whom the copy is required to be filed for
the amount of ten dollars ($10) per day after the deadline
until the statement or report is filed. The officer shall
deposit any funds received under this section into the general
fund of the jurisdiction of which he is an officer. No
liability under this section shall exceed the cumulative
amount stated in the late statement or report, or one hundred
dollars ($100), whichever is greater."

1. Does the Secretary of State have any discretion with
regard to the imposition of a fine pursuant to the
provisions of Government Code Section 91013?

75 106

Opinion Request, Fair Political Practices Commission '•. Page 2

2. Must the Secretary of State give notice to the late
filer before levying this fine? Must there be proof
of the actual receipt of notice prior to the imposition of the fine, or is the mere mailing of the
notice by the filing officer sufficient?

3. Is the filing officer required to impose the penalty
on the day after the actual deadline or on the date
of notice?

Sincerely,[handwritten signature]
MARCH FONG EU
Secretary of State

By: [handwritten text] Betty Jo Smith-Patterson
Legal Counsel

BJSPas
